the same block as createVault"},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. clearBrokenVault(address).details: Allows overwriting a vault that failed to initialize properlyOnly owner can call this function

params.user: User address whose vault should be cleared

params.strategyName: Name of the strategy to create
params.user: User address who will own the vault

returns._0: Address of the user's vault, or 0 if not deployed

getVaultsNeedingUpgrade().details: Useful for identifying vaults that should be upgraded to get bug fixesEach vault owner must call upgradeToLatest() on their vault to upgrade

returns._0: Array of vault addresses that are not on the latest implementation
true if vault is initialized (has owner and strategy), false otherwise

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

predictVaultAddress(address,address,uint.details: Salt includes block.timestamp, so this is only useful within the same block as createVault

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params._implementation: Address of the new AnalogVault implementation

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

transferVaultOwnership(address,address).details: Can only be called by factory owner or controller

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

clearBrokenVault(address).notice: Clear a broken/uninitialized vault from the factory mapping

createVault(address,string).notice: Create a vault and strategy for a user

getVault(address).notice: Get vault address for a user (returns 0 if not deployed)

getVaultCount().notice: Get total number of deployed vaults

getVaultsNeedingUpgrade().notice: Get list of vaults that need upgrading to latest implementation

isVaultInitialized(address).notice: Check if a vault is properly initialized

setController(address).notice: Update controller address for all future vaults

setLatestImplementation(address).notice: Set the latest implementation address for vault upgrades

transferVaultOwnership(address,address).notice: Transfer vault ownership to user (called after initialization)
